X.notes: 22 right-sized tasks. Cleanly splits R001's two deliverables (T001 atomic exclusion + T002 N-thread regression suite), R003 into path/health/docs (T004/T005/T006), R004 into root/surface/token (T007/T008/T009), and R008 into evidence/decisions/deps-gate (T018/T019/T020). Dependency edges all correct and consumer->producer: T002->T001, T006->T004,T005, T011->T010, T015->T010. Criteria are machine-checkable; verification uses consistent, plausible `python -m anvil <cmd>` and pytest paths. No hallucinated scope.

Y.notes: Only 9 tasks - one mega-task per feature. T008 packs all five R008 deliverables (evidence query, overlap surfacing, decision back-prop, atomic dep batch, contract gates) into one task; T005 packs JSON+schema-version+next-task. R001's two deliverables crammed into T001. T001 depends-on T002 is a backwards/spurious edge (claim exclusion does not depend on fractional lease), and several tasks emit empty `**Dependencies:**` fields, violating the omit rule. Faithfulness broken: `cargo test` in a Python (`python -m`/pytest) project, and literal `<...>` template placeholders left in descriptions.

Z.notes: 21 well-sized tasks; correctly splits R001 (T001 exclusion + T003 regression suite) and R008 (T016-T019). Coverage gaps: R003 self-sufficient docs not addressed, R005 authoritative on-disk schema version not a dedicated deliverable, R008 file-overlap evidence surfacing missing (T016 only queries). Dependency_correctness is the weak point: real edges (T003->T001,T002; T005->T004) are right, but many spurious linear chains - T021->T020 (github depends on mermaid), T019->T018->T017 (independent R008 sub-parts), T015->T014->T013 (docker depends on global-config depends on migration) - impose ordering infrastructure does not demand. Some invented `--test` flags slightly weaken verifiability.

rationale: X is the clear winner: 22 right-sized tasks that fully decompose every multi-deliverable requirement (R001's atomic exclusion T001 + standing concurrency suite T002; R004's three sub-parts T007-T009) with only correct, consumer->producer dependency edges. Z is a solid second - good granularity and split (T001+T003 for R001) but loses points for spurious dependency chains (T021->T020, T019->T018, T015->T014->T013) and minor coverage gaps (no R003 docs task, no dedicated on-disk schema-version deliverable). Y is last by a wide margin: 9 mega-tasks that pack whole features into single items (T008 = five R008 deliverables), a backwards T001->T002 edge, empty Dependencies fields, and a `cargo test` command hallucinated into a Python project.
